mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets trigger wage inflation expectations that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the low-unemployment signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while consumer credit expands signals unsustainable debt-fueled demand that will soon face supply constraints, causing negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline + RSAFS expansion) fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic below -1.96, or the joint signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting real GDP growth on equity valuations, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in tightening regimes do not show a statistically significant negative correlation with the combined rate/GDP signal (p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.10 for two consecutive years, or forward returns following payroll growth spikes are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts exceed 1.5 million annualized units, it signals robust consumer confidence and construction activity that spills over into broader economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher following housing starts above 1.5M units compared to baseline periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and rise in core inflation indicates supply-side cost shocks that erode profit margins and compress equity valuations, leading to negative returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the interaction term's Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, accelerating real GDP growth combined with stable short-term rates reduces uncertainty premiums, producing strongly positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns during high-GDP growth periods do not exhibit a positive premium relative to low-growth periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's predictive power vanishes when GDP revisions exceed 1.5% of prior quarter's value.
